task_completion.description: Verify the bcWebCam application is launched.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Options/Settings window is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Options, Settings, or Preferences dialog box for bcWebCam is open and visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration section for post-scan behavior is located.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific tab, menu, or section within the Settings window governing 'actions after reading a barcode' or 'post-scan behavior' is navigated to and visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the option to terminate/exit the application is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox, radio button, or dropdown option specifically labeled to terminate, exit, or close the application after a successful scan is actively selected or checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the changes are applied and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking the 'OK', 'Apply', or 'Save' button in the settings window to confirm the configuration change.

task_completion.description: Verify the application is left running after saving settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final screenshots, the settings window is closed but the main bcWebCam application window remains open and running.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to change settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the bcWebCam GUI menus and dialogs. There should be no evidence of the agent opening configuration files in a text editor to change settings.

integrity.description: Verify interaction is with the genuine bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being configured is clearly bcWebCam, matching standard UI elements for the software, rather than a mock-up or unrelated program.

integrity.description: Verify no terminal commands or scripts were used to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no open terminal windows (like Command Prompt or PowerShell) executing commands or scripts to modify registry keys or application data directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected behavior is 'Terminate application on scan success'. Verify that the specific setting to terminate or exit the application immediately after reading a barcode is explicitly selected in the bcWebCam GUI, and that the application is left running at the end of the task.
